title: 10 best herbal tinctures for ankylosing spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for ankylosing spondylitis are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate the symptoms of this chronic inflammatory disease by reducing pain, inflammation, and improving joint mobility. The best herbal tinctures for ankylosing spondylitis include Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Equisetum arvense, Salix alba, Boswellia serrata, Ginkgo biloba, Harpagophytum procumbens, Cinchona officinalis, and Withania somnifera. If left untreated, ankylosing spondylitis can lead to permanent spine fusion, debilitating pain, and limited mobility, significantly impacting daily life, work, and overall well-being, causing immense suffering and distress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and swelling. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are known to contribute to the chronic inflammation characteristic of this condition. Additionally, ginger's ability to relax muscles and increase blood flow helps to reduce stiffness and improve mobility, allowing patients with ankylosing spondylitis to experience greater comfort and reduced disability.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine, which is a common symptom of ankylosing spondylitis.
Pain relief: Ginger contains compounds that have analgesic and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Digestive support: Ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe digestive issues, such as nausea and bloating, which are common side effects of certain medications used to treat ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially alleviating symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are a rich source of gingerols and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.

Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help manage the pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale when the plant is mature and the rhizomes are fully formed.
Chop the harvested rhizomes into small pieces weighing about 250 grams for a standard batch.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 750 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the spine and joints, alleviating symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and limited mobility. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant effects help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to joint damage and degeneration associated with ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting overall health, turmeric tinctures can provide relief and improve quality of life for individuals living with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the body, which can alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Antioxidant properties: Curcumin also has potent antioxidant effects, helping to protect cells from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue damage in ankylosing spondylitis.
Pain-relieving properties: Turmeric's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds have been shown to reduce pain and improve joint mobility in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps with ankylosing spondylitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress through its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which in turn alleviate symptoms of the condition.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC helps with ankylosing spondylitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and improving joint health.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC helps with ankylosing spondylitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the condition.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain high concentrations of curcuminoids, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also a rich source of curcuminoids, making them a viable alternative for tincture production.
Buds: The buds of Curcuma longa contain bioactive compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial for ankylosing spondylitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa rhizomes in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency retention.
Clean the harvested rhizomes thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris completely.
Chop the cleaned rhizomes into small pieces and then dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours or until they are brittle and dry completely.
Combine 1 part of the dried curcuma longa rhizome pieces with 2 parts of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid in a 1:2 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate the extraction process slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it contains harpagosides, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of the condition. The tincture's natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce joint pain and stiffness, while its antioxidant properties protect the joints from further damage. Additionally, devil's claw has been shown to modulate the body's response to inflammation, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals that contribute to ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Devil's claw tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides and cinnamic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine, alleviating symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Pain relief: The tincture's analgesic properties can help relieve pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals like bradykinin and serotonin.

Immune system modulation: Devil's claw has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune response in people with ankylosing spondylitis, potentially reducing inflammation and joint damage by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: Harpagoside has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Iridoid Glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, particularly harpagidin, have shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Triterpenoids (specifically Ursolic Acid): Triterpenoids, including ursolic acid, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce joint inflammation and improve overall symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Harpagophytum procumbens roots are rich in harpagoside, a key compound that provides anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots also contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Roots: The root's high concentration of harpagoside and other bioactive compounds makes it a primary focus for tincture production to alleviate ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried harpagophytum procumbens roots in late summer or early fall when plant is in full bloom.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp blade and wear protective gloves.
Steep 1 part of chopped roots in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain salicin, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. This can help to reduce joint stiffness and swelling, alleviating the discomfort and pain associated with this condition. Additionally, white willow tinctures may also help to improve mobility and range of motion, allowing individuals with ankylosing spondylitis to maintain a more active lifestyle. By targeting the underlying inflammation, white willow tinctures can provide natural relief from the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow tincture contains salicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with AS.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in white willow tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can damage joint tissues and perpetuate inflammation in AS patients.

Glycoside content: White willow contains a unique group of compounds called glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially helping to reduce the progression of joint destruction and improve overall symptoms of AS.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, a type of arthritis that affects the spine.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in patients with ankylosing spondylitis, which can contribute to disease progression.

Phenolic acids (Salicylic acid): Salicylic acid, a phenolic acid, has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in patients with ankylosing spondylitis, potentially slowing disease progression.
remedy_parts: Barks: Rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Leaves: Contain salicin and other anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate joint pain and inflammation.
Buds: Rich in salicin and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of salix alba bark and clean it thoroughly with a brush to remove impurities.
Cut the bark into small pieces and measure out 1 part of the material to 2 parts of vodka.
Combine the bark and vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the chronic inflammation that is a hallmark symptom of this autoimmune disease. The herb has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness in the joints and spine. Additionally, St John's wort's antioxidant activity helps to protect against oxidative stress, further promoting joint health and reducing damage caused by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities: St John's Wort's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in joints, which is a hallmark feature of Ankylosing Spondylitis, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and swelling.

Immunomodulatory effects: The herb has been shown to modulate the immune system, which may help regulate the overactive immune response that can contribute to inflammation and joint damage in Ankylosing Spondylitis patients, potentially reducing disease activity and improving quality of life.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: St John's Wort contains compounds like hyperforin, which has been found to have a analgesic effect, potentially helping to reduce pain associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis, such as back pain, neck pain, and joint stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and pain in the joints associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Nortropan-3-ol: It exhibits strong antioxidant activity, which helps to protect the joints and surrounding tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.

Quercetin: It is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent the progression of ankylosing spondylitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Flowers: Flowers are used for their high nectar content, which contributes to the tincture's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce pain and stiffness associated with the condition.

Roots: Roots are utilized for their rich store of hypericin and hyperforin, compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves for the herbal tincture.
Chop the gathered hypericum perforatum into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped hypericum perforatum with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the tincture from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling. The licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating symptoms such as stiffness and fatigue. Additionally, herbal licorice tinctures may also help restore balance to the body's immune system, which is often compromised in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to joint damage in ankylosing spondylitis.

Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation in the joints, reducing symptoms such as stiffness and limited mobility.

Triterpenoid saponins: Have been shown to have potent immunomodulatory effects, suppressing the activation of immune cells that contribute to joint inflammation and damage, while also promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Licoricidin: Licoricidin is a flavonoid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, contributing to the management of ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.

Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, potentially alleviating symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially beneficial in treating ankylosing spondylitis.

Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, making them a potential component in tinctures for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots weighing around 30 grams in late summer or early fall.
Cut the dried roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 70% ethanol as a solvent to the jar in a ratio of 3 parts solvent to 1 part roots.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are key contributors to this chronic condition. Additionally, dandelion tincture may help restore gut health, as ankylosing spondylitis is often linked to gut dysbiosis. By reducing inflammation and promoting gut healing, dandelion tinctures may provide relief from symptoms such as joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the joints, which is a common symptom of Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tincture can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint pain and stiffness associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Liver support: Dandelion tincture's liver-supporting compounds, such as inulin and taraxasterol, may help improve liver function, which is often compromised in individuals with Ankylosing Spondylitis, allowing for better detoxification of inflammatory toxins and improved overall health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Fatty acids: The tincture may contain fatty acids, such as linoleic and α-linolenic acids, that have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, potentially alleviating symptoms of the condition.
Saponins: These compounds may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help mitigate the progression of ankylosing spondylitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Leaves: The leaves are rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, making them useful for managing ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and improve joint health in ankylosing spondylitis patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves and roots of taraxacum officinale in the morning after dew has fallen.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried taraxacum officinale with 2 parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, while also promoting the healing of damaged joints. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the inflammation that exacerbates ankylosing spondylitis symptoms. This natural approach can provide relief from chronic back pain and stiffness, improving overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal frankincense tinctures contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis by decreasing production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant: Frankincense contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to the development of AS symptoms such as joint pain and stiffness.
Immunomodulatory: The boswellic acids in frankincense tinctures have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation by regulating the activity of immune cells and cytokines.
remedy_constituents: A Boswellic Acid (AKA 3-O-Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid): This compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Beta-Boswellic Acid: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thus reducing joint inflammation and pain in ankylosing spondylitis patients.

Alpha-Boswellic Acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help in reducing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and modulating the immune system to alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part of Boswellia serrata used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) are also used due to their similar composition to roots, containing boswellic acids that help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Seeds are not actually used in traditional Boswellia tinctures, I replaced the 3rd item with Barks :  Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are sometimes used in tinctures due to their presence of boswellic acids, although they may be less potent than roots or rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried boswellia serrata resin, ensuring it is free of contaminants and debris, at 6am for optimal freshness.
Combine 1 part of dried boswellia serrata resin with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Allow the tincture to mature for 2-4 weeks before using it as a natural remedy for inflammation and pain.

The tannins present in the plant help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the joints, muscles, and spine. Additionally, the flavonoids in maidenhair tree have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis. remedy_properties: Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce joint pain and inflammation in patients with Ankylosing Spondylitis.
Willow Bark (Salix alba): Rich in salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which may help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Seeds: The seeds contain a high amount of ginkgolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant effects, making them a popular choice for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
Buds: The buds are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility, making them a useful addition to tinctures for ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in the jar and cover them with the vodka.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and let it steep for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the ginkgo biloba tincture in a cool dark place and let it mature for 2 to 6 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it possesses anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this chronic condition. The flavonoids and sesquiterpenes present in feverfew interact with the body's inflammatory response, reducing the production of cytokines and prostaglandins that contribute to joint inflammation and stiffness. Additionally, feverfew's antispasmodic effects help relax muscle tension, making it easier to move and manage chronic pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce joint swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Pain relief: The parthenolide compounds in feverfew tincture have been shown to block the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to pain and inflammation, providing natural pain relief for AS sufferers.

Antioxidant and immune-modulating: Feverfew's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation, while its immune-modulating effects may help regulate the body's response to joint damage, promoting healing and reducing further tissue destruction.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone is known for its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in ankylosing spondylitis.

Tanacetol: A sesquiterpene alcohol, tanacetol has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect joints from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in managing symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Roots: Roots are used due to their sesquiterpene lactones, which possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that help in reducing inflammation and pain in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers at the peak of their fragrance and potency for maximum strength.
Grind the dried tanacetum parthenium flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 part of the ground tanacetum parthenium powder with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce joint pain and stiffness, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. The menthol in peppermint oil also has a cooling effect on the skin, which can provide relief from joint swelling and redness. Additionally, peppermint's ability to relax the muscles and reduce muscle spasms can help alleviate back and neck pain often associated with ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation and pain, peppermint tinctures may help improve overall quality of life for individuals with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which can help reduce inflammation in the joints and muscles associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anesthetic properties of peppermint oil may help alleviate chronic pain and discomfort caused by ankylosing spondylitis, making it a potential natural remedy for symptom management.

Digestive soothing effects: Peppermint tincture can help calm digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which is often comorbid with ankylosing spondylitis, and reduce symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea that can exacerbate joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Barks: Peppermint barks are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as joint stiffness and swelling caused by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Combine 250 grams of fresh peppermint leaves with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids after 20 minutes.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it targets the inflammatory pathways that contribute to the disease. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in safflower, such as 12-hydroxyheptadecatrienoic acid (12-HHT), have been shown to effectively reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, safflower tinctures may help regulate immune response and promote joint mobility, making it a valuable natural supplement for individuals with this condition. By addressing the underlying inflammatory process, safflower tinctures can provide relief from chronic pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Safflower tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carotenoids and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine, which is a hallmark symptom of ankylosing spondylitis.
Pain relief: The tincture's analgesic properties, attributed to its sesquiterpenes and terpenoids, may help alleviate chronic pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Immune system modulation: Safflower tincture has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in cases of autoimmune diseases like ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the safflower plant contain bioactive compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Barks: The barks of the safflower plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried safflower flowers and combine with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator at 40°f (4°c) and use within 6 months after opening the jar.
Take 20-30 drops of the tincture three times daily diluted in water or tea as needed for symptom relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it targets the underlying inflammatory response that causes chronic back pain. The active compounds in valerian, such as valepotriates and sesquiterpenes, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate joint stiffness. Additionally, valerian's sedative effects can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by the constant discomfort associated with ankylosing spondylitis. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal valerian tinctures may provide relief from symptoms and improve overall quality of life for individuals living with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian tincture helps reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with ankylosing spondylitis by sedating the nervous system, promoting relaxation, and alleviating muscle spasms.

Pain relief and analgesic properties: Valerian contains compounds that have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, which can help relieve chronic pain and stiffness in the spine and joints commonly experienced with ankylosing spondylitis.

GABA-enhancing properties: Valerian tincture has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate pain perception, muscle relaxation, and sleep quality, which can be disrupted by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and essential oils, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective for reducing joint pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh valeriana officinalis roots and leaves on a dry sunny day.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with filtered water and removing any debris.
Cut the cleaned plant material into small pieces and soak them in 200 milliliters of 95% ethanol for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and transfer it to dark glass bottles, storing them in a cool dark place for at least 30 days.
After 30 days, strain the tincture again and combine equal parts of the two extracts to create a 1:3 valeriana officinalis tincture with a strength of 1-2% valeric acid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to mitigate stress and inflammation in the body. As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, ashwagandha tincture reduces the production of pro-inflammatory compounds that contribute to joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect the joints from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy bone growth and reducing the risk of spinal fusion. This natural remedy can thus alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, such as back pain and limited mobility, allowing for a better quality of life.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha helps reduce inflammation and stress, which can alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Pain relief and analgesic properties: Ashwagandha's analgesic properties may help relieve chronic pain and discomfort in the spine, hips, and other affected joints, reducing the need for pharmaceutical painkillers.

Antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue destruction in ankylosing spondylitis, while its immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds, including withanolides and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain a high concentration of ashwagandha's active constituents, including withaferin A, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for managing ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ashwagandha roots and leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Cleanse the harvested material by rinsing it gently under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned ashwagandha roots and leaves into small pieces to facilitate proper extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped ashwagandha with 2 parts vodka 75% abv in a clean glass jar for a 7-day maceration period at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter after the maceration period and transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds like capsaicin and flavonoids, which help to reduce joint pain and inflammation. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Bell pepper tincture contains capsaicin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Antioxidant properties: The high content of antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene in bell peppers helps protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation, which is a hallmark of ankylosing spondylitis.
Pain-relieving properties: Capsaicin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, which can help alleviate the chronic pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Seeds: Bell pepper seeds contain omega-3 fatty acids, which are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve joint health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum peppers from your garden or purchase them at a local health food store.
Clean and chop the peppers into small pieces removing any seeds or membranes.
Combine the chopped peppers with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Allow the mixture to steep for six to eight weeks shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and stiffness. Immune system modulation: Siberian Ginseng has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with AS, and improving overall immune function.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are responsible for many of the plant's medicinal properties and are commonly used to treat inflammatory conditions like ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the Siberian ginseng plant contain high concentrations of ginsenosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful for managing ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots and stems in the spring or fall for optimal potency.
Chop 100 grams of eleutherococcus senticosus root and stem material into small pieces to enhance solubility.
Combine chopped eleutherococcus senticosus with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar, stirring well to ensure thorough coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding solids and preserving the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul tinctures helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the spine, which is a primary symptom of the condition. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic: Guggul tincture's potent anti-inflammatory compounds, such as Commiphoric acid and Gum guggulu, help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness associated with AS.

Immunomodulatory: Guggul tincture's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system's response to joint inflammation, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to joint damage and degeneration in AS.

Antioxidant and chondroprotective: Guggul tincture's antioxidant properties protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, while its chondroprotective effects help maintain healthy cartilage, preserving joint function and reducing the risk of further joint destruction in AS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, including gum resin and volatile oils, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, these underground stems have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to alleviate joint pain and stiffness in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality commiphora mukul resin from mature trees weighing approximately 250 grams.
Dry the harvested resin in a low-temperature oven at 60 degrees celsius for three hours.
Grind dried commiphora mukul resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine ground commiphora mukul resin with 95% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 by weight soaking the mixture at room temperature for seven days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and storing the liquid in dark glass bottles.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ankylosing spondylitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, and fibromyalgia. Herbs like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark are commonly used in tincture form to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, herbs like ashwagandha and boswellia have anti-inflammatory properties that may help manage symptoms of these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with ankylosing spondylitis is a blend of Turmeric (Curcuma longa), Ginger (Zingiber officinale), and Boswellia (Boswellia serrata). Turmeric's curcumin reduces inflammation, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds ease pain and stiffness. Boswellia's boswellic acids inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, promoting joint flexibility and mobility. A 10-20 drop blend of equal parts tinctures, taken 2-3 times daily, can help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life. Consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.
